name: node-regjsparser
title: node-regjsparser
summary: Parser for JavaScript's RegExp for Node.js
description:
Parser for JavaScript's RegExp for Node.js
This module provides a parser for the JavaScript's RegExp, itself written
in Javascript.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use regjsparser because Parser for JavaScript's RegExp for Node.js.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use regjsparser as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `regjsparser [regex ...]` as documented upstream for regjsparser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `regjsparser [-f | --flags] [flags] [regex ...]` as documented upstream for regjsparser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `regjsparser [-v | --version]` as documented upstream for regjsparser.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `regjsparser [-h | --help]` as documented upstream for regjsparser.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair regjsparser with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `regjsparser --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: regjsparser - Parser of Javascript regular expressions
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-regjsparser. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `regjsparser [regex ...]`. Further invocations from upstream: `regjsparser [-f | --flags] [flags] [regex ...]`; `regjsparser [-v | --version]`. Parser for JavaScript's RegExp for Node.js.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Parser for JavaScript's RegExp for Node.js.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: regjsparser - Parser of Javascript regular expressions.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install node-regjsparser
